executiveSummary: This project aims to consolidate our 5 disparate regional payment processors into a single, global payment gateway (Stripe). This consolidation will reduce processing fees, simplify financial reconciliation, and allow us to easily enter new international markets.

businessGoals.strategicAlignment: Corporate OKR: Improve operational profit margins.
businessGoals.successMetric: Decrease average transaction fee from 2.9% to 2.1% globally.

businessGoals.strategicAlignment: Corporate OKR: Launch in 3 new EMEA markets in 2027.
businessGoals.successMetric: Ability to accept local payment methods (e.g., iDEAL, Bancontact) with zero additional engineering effort.

inScope: Migration of all active North American and European customer billing to the new gateway.
Implementation of automated daily reconciliation reports for the Finance team.
outOfScope: Migration of legacy, inactive accounts (canceled prior to 2025).

stakeholders.name: Sarah Jenkins
coreExpectations: No disruption to monthly revenue recognition workflows
Automated mapping of transaction fees to the general ledger

stakeholders.name: Marcus Thorne
coreExpectations: Clear API documentation from the chosen vendor
Budget allocation for 3 backend engineers for 2 sprints

highLevelRequirements.description: The system must support automatic currency conversion and settlement in USD, EUR, and GBP.
The chosen solution must be PCI-DSS Level 1 compliant, offloading all credit card data storage from our internal servers.
Customer Support agents must have a unified dashboard to issue refunds without needing engineering assistance.

financials.expectedFinancialBenefit: $850,000 annual savings in processing fees and reduced engineering maintenance.

businessAssumptions: The current payment volume ($100M/year) will remain stable or grow, ensuring we qualify for the vendor's enterprise discount tier.
resourceConstraints: Project must be completed using existing internal engineering resources; no external contractors.
scheduleConstraints: Must be fully deployed and tested prior to the October 1st code freeze ahead of the holiday shopping season.
